titleStmt.title: Letter from Mick Timoney to Charlie Daly
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Mick Timoney

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

correspAction.placeName: 2249 G Block, Military Barracks, Newbridge, Kildare, Ireland
Drumboe Castle, Stranorlar, Tirconail, Donegal, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
P41/2/21/10 Mick Timoney
Prisoner
2249 G Block
Military Camp
New Bridge
Co Kildare Dear Charles Just a few lines hoping
to find you all in good health as this
leaves me at present. I hope you will
excuse me for neglecting to write for such
a long time s I was not quite sure
that yous were sil still in Drumboe.
There is not much interesting here
be remembered to
to talk about all the boys wishes to
you all to told Ginger told me he
would be writing to some of you
shortly but as we are only allowed to
write and recieve two letters each
week I suppose he has got his comple-
ment out. Frank Smith Sion Mills
wishes to be remembered to James
Lane. I think I will finish up now
hoping to hear from you, soon, and
wishing to be remembered to all,
Good bye for the present. I remain Your Sincere Friend
Mick Timoney
